title: DeepCAD: A Deep Generative Network for Computer-Aided Design Models
abstract: Deep generative models of 3D shapes have received a great deal of research interest. Yet, almost all of them generate discrete shape representations, such as voxels, point clouds, and polygon meshes. We present the first 3D generative model that directly outputs shapes as sequences of CAD operations. Drawing parallels to natural language, we propose a Transformer-based network for CAD generation. We demonstrate capabilities in shape autoencoding and random generation, supported by a new dataset of 178,238 CAD models with construction sequences.
method_summary: Transformer-based autoregressive model that represents 3D shapes as sequences of CAD operations (sketch-and-extrude). Uses an encoder-decoder architecture to learn a latent space of CAD command sequences for both autoencoding and unconditional generation.

title: SkexGen: Autoregressive Generation of CAD Construction Sequences with Disentangled Codebooks
abstract: We present SkexGen, a novel autoregressive generative model for computer-aided design (CAD) construction sequences containing sketch-and-extrude modeling operations. Our model utilizes distinct Transformer architectures to encode topological, geometric, and extrusion variations of construction sequences into disentangled codebooks. Autoregressive Transformer decoders generate CAD sequences with specified properties, demonstrating capabilities in producing diverse, high-quality models while enhancing user control and enabling efficient design space exploration.
method_summary: Uses VQ-VAE with disentangled codebooks (topology, geometry, extrusion) to encode CAD construction sequences. Cascaded autoregressive Transformer decoders generate sequences with controllable properties from sampled codes.

title: Hierarchical Neural Coding for Controllable CAD Model Generation
abstract: We present a generative model that represents high-level design concepts of a CAD model as a three-level hierarchical tree of neural codes, from global part arrangement down to local curve geometry. A novel variant of a vector quantized VAE with masked skip connection extracts design variations as neural codebooks at three levels. Two-stage cascaded auto-regressive transformers learn to generate and complete CAD models from incomplete inputs.
method_summary: Three-level hierarchical VQ-VAE with masked skip connections encodes CAD designs into neural codebooks at part-arrangement, sketch, and curve levels. Cascaded autoregressive transformers enable both random generation and conditional completion.

title: CAD-SIGNet: CAD Language Inference from Point Clouds using Layer-wise Sketch Instance Guided Attention
abstract: We propose CAD-SIGNet, an end-to-end trainable and auto-regressive architecture to recover the design history of a CAD model represented as a sequence of sketch-and-extrusion from an input point cloud. Our model learns CAD visual-language representations by layer-wise cross-attention between point cloud and CAD language embedding. A new Sketch instance Guided Attention (SGA) module reconstructs fine-grained sketch details. The auto-regressive approach enables both full design history recovery and conditional auto-completion.
method_summary: End-to-end autoregressive architecture using layer-wise cross-attention between point cloud features and CAD language embeddings. Novel Sketch instance Guided Attention (SGA) module for fine-grained sketch reconstruction from 3D point clouds.

title: SketchGen: Generating Constrained CAD Sketches
abstract: Computer-aided design (CAD) is the most widely used modeling approach for technical design. The typical starting point is 2D sketches which can later be extruded and combined to obtain complex 3D assemblies. Such sketches are composed of parametric primitives augmented with geometric constraints. We introduce SketchGen, a transformer-based generative model that designs a sequential language for primitives and constraints, allowing distinguishing between different types and their parameters. The model can predict constraints for given primitives and generate complete sketches from scratch.
method_summary: Transformer-based autoregressive model with a custom sequential language for CAD sketch primitives and geometric constraints. Handles heterogeneous primitive types and constraint relationships in a unified sequence format.

key_results.improvement: significant over prior CAD sketch generation

title: Vitruvion: A Generative Model of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: Parametric CAD tools encode design intent through geometric primitives and parameterized constraints between elements. This relational specification functions as a constraint program, allowing edits to propagate coherently. We present a generative modeling approach for parametric CAD sketches trained on real-world designs from the SketchGraphs dataset. Our model autoregressively synthesizes sketches as sequences of primitives with initial coordinates and constraints referencing back to sampled primitives.
method_summary: Autoregressive transformer that generates parametric CAD sketches as sequences of primitives and constraints. Directly outputs constraint graph representations compatible with standard CAD software for downstream editing.

title: Mamba-CAD: State Space Model For 3D Computer-Aided Design Generative Modeling
abstract: We introduce Mamba-CAD, a self-supervised generative modeling approach for complex industrial CAD models that can model longer parametric CAD sequences. The approach uses an encoder-decoder framework based on a Mamba architecture paired with self-supervised pre-training on CAD reconstruction. A generative adversarial network then produces synthetic representations decoded into parametric sequences. We created a dataset of 77,078 CAD models and demonstrated effectiveness for generating valid longer sequences.
method_summary: Mamba-based encoder-decoder with self-supervised pre-training for CAD sequence reconstruction. Uses GAN for generation of latent representations that are decoded into parametric CAD command sequences, handling longer sequences than Transformer-based methods.

title: MamTiff-CAD: Multi-Scale Latent Diffusion with Mamba+ for Complex Parametric Sequence
abstract: We propose MamTiff-CAD, which leverages a Transformer-based diffusion model for multi-scale latent representations. The framework combines Mamba+ and Transformer in a custom autoencoder where the Mamba+ block incorporates a forget gate mechanism to effectively capture long-range dependencies. A non-autoregressive Transformer decoder reconstructs the latent representations. A diffusion model trained on latent embeddings learns distributions for extended command sequences up to 256 commands per model.
method_summary: Hybrid Mamba+/Transformer autoencoder for multi-scale latent CAD representations. Mamba+ with forget gate captures long-range dependencies; diffusion model generates in latent space for sequences of 60-256 commands.

title: GeoFusion-CAD: Structure-Aware Diffusion with Geometric State Space for Parametric 3D Design
abstract: We propose GeoFusion-CAD, an end-to-end diffusion framework for scalable and structure-aware generation. We encode CAD programs as hierarchical trees, jointly capturing geometry and topology. A C-Mamba block models long-range structural dependencies through selective state transitions. We introduce DeepCAD-240, extending sequence lengths from 40 to 240 commands while preserving sketch-extrusion semantics. The approach achieves superior performance on both short and long command ranges, maintaining high geometric fidelity and topological consistency where Transformer-based models degrade.
method_summary: End-to-end diffusion framework encoding CAD programs as hierarchical trees with C-Mamba blocks for long-range structural dependencies via selective state transitions. Introduces DeepCAD-240 benchmark extending sequences to 240 commands.

title: SketchDNN: Joint Continuous-Discrete Diffusion for CAD Sketch Generation
abstract: We present SketchDNN, a generative model for synthesizing CAD sketches that jointly models both continuous parameters and discrete class labels through a unified continuous-discrete diffusion process. The key innovation is Gaussian-Softmax diffusion, which applies Gaussian noise to logits and projects them onto the probability simplex via softmax, enabling blended class labels for discrete variables. Results show FID decreased from 16.04 to 7.80 and NLL from 84.8 to 81.33 on SketchGraphs.
method_summary: Unified continuous-discrete diffusion using Gaussian-Softmax noise process for CAD sketch generation. Handles heterogeneous primitive parameterizations and permutation invariance by jointly diffusing continuous coordinates and discrete type labels.

title: DreamCAD: Scaling Multi-modal CAD Generation using Differentiable Parametric Surfaces
abstract: We propose DreamCAD, which directly produces editable BReps from point-level supervision, without CAD-specific annotations. The framework represents boundary representations as parametric patches and employs differentiable tessellation for mesh generation, enabling training on large-scale 3D datasets. We introduce CADCap-1M, the largest CAD captioning dataset with 1M+ descriptions generated using GPT-5. The method achieves state-of-the-art results on ABC and Objaverse benchmarks across text, image, and point modalities.
method_summary: Represents BReps as differentiable parametric patches (Bezier surfaces) with differentiable tessellation enabling training on mesh datasets without CAD annotations. Multi-modal conditioning via text, image, and point cloud inputs. Introduces CADCap-1M captioning dataset.

title: GenCAD: Image-Conditioned Computer-Aided Design Generation with Transformer-Based Contrastive Representation and Diffusion Priors
abstract: We introduce GenCAD, a generative model that employs autoregressive transformers with a contrastive learning framework and latent diffusion models to transform image inputs into parametric CAD command sequences. The approach generates editable 3D shape representations, outperforming existing methods in both unconditional and conditional CAD generation, and enables retrieval of CAD models using image queries from large CAD databases.
method_summary: Combines autoregressive transformer with contrastive learning and latent diffusion priors for image-to-CAD generation. Uses CLIP-like contrastive training to align image and CAD sequence representations, then diffusion model generates in latent space.

title: RECAD: Revisiting CAD Model Generation by Learning Raster Sketch
abstract: We introduce RECAD, a framework that generates CAD models by producing raster sketches and 3D extrusions. Rather than using discrete sequences of parametric segments, the approach represents sketches as raster images, offering enhanced geometric representation without constraints on line/curve types, interpolation within continuous latent space, and more intuitive user control. Two diffusion networks generate extrusion boxes and sketch images respectively.
method_summary: Dual diffusion network framework: first network generates extrusion boxes conditioned on parameters, second produces raster sketch images conditioned on boxes. Represents CAD sketches as raster images instead of parametric sequences for more flexible geometry.

title: FlexCAD: Unified and Versatile Controllable CAD Generation with Fine-tuned Large Language Models
abstract: We propose FlexCAD, which fine-tunes large language models for controllable CAD generation. The approach represents CAD models as structured text, abstracting each hierarchy as token sequences. Hierarchy-aware masking during training masks specific CAD text fields and trains the LLM to predict them. At inference, user intent converts to CAD text with masked sections. Supports control across sketch-extrusion, extrusion, sketch, face, loop and curve levels.
method_summary: Fine-tunes LLMs on CAD models represented as structured text. Hierarchy-aware masking strategy enables controllable generation at multiple CAD construction levels (sketch-extrusion, extrusion, sketch, face, loop, curve) within a single unified model.

title: Feasibility Enhancement for 3D CAD Generation
abstract: This framework integrates a guided diffusion denoising process in the latent space and a regression-based correction mechanism to refine infeasible CAD command sequences while preserving geometric accuracy. By significantly improving the feasibility rate of generating CAD models, our approach helps expand the availability of high-quality training data and enhances the applicability of AI-driven CAD generation.
method_summary: Post-processing framework combining guided diffusion denoising in latent space with regression-based correction to fix infeasible CAD command sequences. Improves validity rate of generated CAD models without sacrificing geometric accuracy.

title: BrepGen: A B-rep Generative Diffusion Model with Structured Latent Geometry
abstract: We present BrepGen, a diffusion-based generative approach that directly outputs Boundary representation (B-rep) CAD models. BrepGen represents a B-rep model as a novel structured latent geometry in a hierarchical tree. With the root node representing a whole CAD solid, each element (face, edge, vertex) stores global bounding box information and latent codes for local geometry. Topology is implicitly captured through node duplication. Transformer-based diffusion sequentially denoises node features while detecting and merging duplicates to recover B-rep topology.
method_summary: Hierarchical tree representation of B-rep with latent geometry codes per face/edge/vertex. Transformer-based diffusion denoises node features; topology recovered by detecting and merging duplicate nodes. First to incorporate free-form and doubly-curved surfaces in CAD generation.

key_results.novelty: first free-form surfaces in CAD generation

title: HiDiGen: Hierarchical Diffusion for B-Rep Generation with Explicit Topological Constraints
abstract: We propose HiDiGen, a two-stage hierarchical framework that decouples geometry modeling while maintaining explicit topological constraints. The first stage establishes face-edge incidence relations and generates face proxies and initial edge curves. The second stage employs Transformer-based diffusion modules to refine geometry by generating precise face surfaces and vertex positions, with edge-vertex adjacencies dynamically enforced.
method_summary: Two-stage hierarchical diffusion: Stage 1 generates topology (face-edge incidence) and coarse geometry; Stage 2 refines with Transformer-based diffusion for precise surfaces and vertices with dynamically enforced topological constraints.

title: DTGBrepGen: A Novel B-rep Generative Model through Decoupling Topology and Geometry
abstract: We propose DTGBrepGen, a novel topology-geometry decoupled framework for B-rep generation. Our approach first generates valid topological structures through a two-stage process modeling adjacency relationships, then uses Transformer-based diffusion models to sequentially generate vertex coordinates, edge geometries, and face geometries as B-splines.
method_summary: Decoupled topology-geometry framework: first generates valid topological structures via two sequential Transformer encoder-decoders for adjacency relations, then Transformer-based diffusion generates vertex/edge/face geometries as B-splines.

title: AutoBrep: Autoregressive B-Rep Generation with Unified Topology and Geometry
abstract: We present AutoBrep, a novel Transformer model that autoregressively generates B-Reps with high quality and validity. AutoBrep employs a unified tokenization scheme that encodes both geometric and topological characteristics of a B-Rep model as a sequence of discrete tokens. Geometric primitives are encoded as latent geometry tokens, and structural relationships as special topological reference tokens. Sequence order follows breadth-first traversal of the B-Rep face adjacency graph.
method_summary: Decoder-only Transformer with unified tokenization encoding both geometry (latent tokens) and topology (reference tokens) of B-rep models. Breadth-first traversal ordering enables progressive face-by-face generation with native autocomplete support.

title: HoLa: B-Rep Generation using a Holistic Latent Representation
abstract: We introduce a novel representation for learning and generating CAD models as boundary representations (B-Reps). The key innovation is a holistic latent (HoLa) space that unifies continuous geometric properties of B-Rep primitives and their discrete topological relations. We observe that the topological connection between two surfaces is intrinsically tied to the geometry of their intersecting curve, enabling reformulation of topology learning as geometric reconstruction. The compact representation supports a diffusion-based generator accepting point clouds, images, sketches, and text prompts, achieving 82% validity vs ~50% for prior work.
method_summary: Holistic latent space unifying B-rep geometry and topology by deriving curve geometries from surface pairs via neural intersection network. Eliminates explicit curves/vertices/topology from latent space. Diffusion-based generator supports multi-modal conditioning.

title: BrepGPT: Autoregressive B-rep Generation with Voronoi Half-Patch
abstract: We present BrepGPT, a single-stage autoregressive framework for B-rep generation. Our key innovation is the Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation, which decomposes B-reps into unified local units by assigning geometry to nearest half-edges and sampling their next pointers. Dual VQ-VAEs encode vertex topology and Voronoi Half-Patches into vertex-based tokens. A decoder-only Transformer autoregressively predicts these tokens for complete B-rep generation.
method_summary: Single-stage autoregressive B-rep generation using Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation that unifies geometry and topology in local units. Dual VQ-VAEs encode vertex topology and patches; decoder-only Transformer generates token sequences.

title: BrepARG: AutoRegressive Generation with B-rep Holistic Token Sequence Representation
abstract: We propose BrepARG, the first attempt to encode B-rep geometry and topology into a holistic token sequence representation, enabling sequence-based B-rep generation with an autoregressive architecture. Three token types (geometry, position, face index) are organized hierarchically. A transformer-based decoder-only model with causal masking learns token distributions through next-token prediction.
method_summary: Encodes complete B-rep geometry and topology into holistic token sequences with three token types (geometry, position, face index). Decoder-only Transformer with causal masking generates B-reps via next-token prediction.

title: Flatten The Complex: Joint B-Rep Generation via Compositional k-Cell Particles
abstract: We introduce a novel paradigm that reformulates B-Reps into sets of compositional k-cell particles. Our approach encodes each topological entity as a composition of particles, where adjacent cells share identical latents at their interfaces, promoting geometric coupling along shared boundaries. By decoupling the rigid hierarchy, our representation unifies vertices, edges, and faces, enabling joint generation of topology and geometry with global context awareness. Multi-modal flow matching framework handles unconditional and conditional tasks.
method_summary: Reformulates B-reps as compositional k-cell particles where adjacent cells share latents at interfaces. Multi-modal flow matching generates particle sets jointly, enabling topology-geometry co-generation with global context. Supports non-manifold structures.

title: CSGNet: Neural Shape Parser for Constructive Solid Geometry
abstract: We present a neural architecture that takes as input a 2D or 3D shape and outputs a program that generates the shape using constructive solid geometry principles. Rather than relying on bottom-up primitive detection, the model employs a recurrent neural network for top-down shape parsing, delivering faster results and more interpretable modeling instructions. The approach can be trained on novel datasets lacking ground-truth program annotations through policy gradient techniques.
method_summary: RNN-based encoder-decoder that parses 2D/3D shapes into CSG programs (boolean operations on primitives). Top-down approach using recurrent generation of CSG instructions. Trainable without program annotations via policy gradient.

title: CSGNe: Neural Shape Parsers for Constructive Solid Geometry
abstract: Constructive Solid Geometry (CSG) is a geometric modeling technique that defines complex shapes by recursively applying boolean operations on primitives. We present CSGNe, a deep network that takes as input a 2D or 3D shape and outputs a CSG program. We explore two architectures: a standard CNN-RNN encoder-decoder and an enhanced version with a memory module based on program execution stacks.
method_summary: Extended CSGNet with memory module based on program execution stacks for improved CSG program generation. CNN-RNN architecture with stack-based memory enables more complex shape decompositions.

title: UCSG-Net: Unsupervised Discovering of Constructive Solid Geometry Tree
abstract: We propose UCSG-Net, a model that extracts a CSG parse tree without any supervision. The approach predicts parameters of primitives, binarizes their signed distance representations through a differentiable indicator function, and simultaneously discovers the Boolean operator tree structure. The model dynamically selects operator combinations that best reconstruct the input.
method_summary: Unsupervised CSG tree discovery using differentiable indicator functions on primitive SDFs. Dynamically selects boolean operator combinations without ground-truth CSG annotations. Produces interpretable parse trees compatible with CAD software.

title: CSG-Stump: A Learning Friendly CSG-Like Representation for Interpretable Shape Parsing
abstract: We present CSG-Stump Net, an unsupervised end-to-end network for learning 3D shapes from point clouds. CSG-Stump is a three-level structure combining complement, intersection, and union layers. It is proven equivalent to CSG in representation, inheriting interpretable, compact and editable nature while freeing from complex tree structures.
method_summary: Three-level CSG-like representation (complement-intersection-union) that is provably equivalent to CSG trees but with constant-dimensionality output. Unsupervised learning from point clouds without CSG annotations.

title: D2CSG: Unsupervised Learning of Compact CSG Trees with Dual Complements and Dropouts
abstract: We present D2CSG, a neural model with two dual and complementary network branches with dropouts for unsupervised learning of compact CSG representations. The network reconstructs shapes through fixed-order assembly of quadric primitives, with a dedicated residual branch for shape complement subtraction. Weight dropout enhances CSG tree compactness by eliminating redundant primitives.
method_summary: Dual-branch neural network for unsupervised CSG learning: cover branch models overall shape, residual branch handles complex complement via subtraction. Dropout mechanism eliminates redundant primitives for compact CSG trees. Provably general representation.

title: CADDreamer: CAD Object Generation from Single-view Images
abstract: We introduce CADDreamer, a novel approach for generating boundary representations (B-rep) of CAD objects from a single image. CADDreamer employs a primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model that captures both local geometric details and high-level structural semantics. By encoding primitive information into color channels, the method leverages pre-trained diffusion priors to infer multi-view normal and semantic maps. Geometric optimization and topology-preserving extraction produce compact CAD objects with sharp edges and watertight topology.
method_summary: Primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model for single-image to B-rep generation. Encodes primitive semantics in color channels to leverage pre-trained diffusion priors. Topology-preserving extraction produces watertight B-rep CAD objects.

title: NURBGen: High-Fidelity Text-to-CAD Generation through LLM-Driven NURBS Modeling
abstract: We present NURBGen, the first framework for generating editable 3D CAD models directly from natural language using NURBS. The system fine-tunes a large language model to convert text into JSON representations containing NURBS surface parameters (control points, knot vectors, degrees, rational weights) convertible to BRep format. A hybrid representation combines untrimmed NURBS with analytic primitives. We introduce partABC, a curated subset of ABC with individual CAD components.
method_summary: LLM fine-tuned to generate NURBS surface parameters (control points, knots, degrees, weights) as JSON from text descriptions. Hybrid representation combining untrimmed NURBS with analytic primitives for trimmed surfaces. Introduces partABC dataset.

title: Img2CAD: Conditioned 3D CAD Model Generation from Single Image with Structured Visual Geometry
abstract: We identify an innovative intermediate representation called Structured Visual Geometry (SVG), characterized by vectorized wireframes extracted from objects. This representation significantly enhances the generation of editable CAD models from single images. Unlike existing AI methods that rely on mesh-based representations incompatible with CAD tools, Img2CAD generates parametric CAD models with editability and fine control.
method_summary: Uses Structured Visual Geometry (SVG) as intermediate representation - vectorized wireframes extracted from images. Two-stage pipeline: image to SVG wireframe, then SVG to parametric CAD model. Bridges the gap between image input and editable CAD output.

title: Masked BRep Autoencoder via Hierarchical Graph Transformer
abstract: We introduce a novel self-supervised learning framework that automatically learns representations from input computer-aided design (CAD) models for downstream tasks, including part classification, model retrieval, and segmentation. The framework uses masked autoencoding on B-rep structures with a hierarchical graph transformer architecture.
method_summary: Self-supervised masked autoencoding framework for B-rep CAD models using hierarchical graph transformer. Learns general-purpose B-rep representations for downstream tasks (classification, retrieval, segmentation) without task-specific supervision.

title: CADFusion: Text-to-CAD Generation Through Infusing Visual Feedback in Large Language Models
abstract: We introduce CADFusion, a framework that uses Large Language Models as the backbone and alternates between two training stages: the sequential learning (SL) stage and the visual feedback (VF) stage. The SL stage trains the LLM to generate parametric CAD sequences from text. The VF stage renders generated models and uses visual comparison to refine generation quality.
method_summary: LLM-based text-to-CAD with alternating sequential learning and visual feedback training stages. Sequential stage learns text-to-CAD-sequence mapping; visual feedback stage renders outputs and uses visual comparison to improve geometric accuracy.

key_results.approach: visual feedback loop for LLM-based CAD

title: Img2CAD: Reverse Engineering 3D CAD Models from Images through VLM-Assisted Conditional Factorization
abstract: We present Img2CAD, a framework for reverse engineering 3D CAD models from single-view images. Our method leverages GPT-4V to predict the discrete CAD program structure and then uses a semantic-conditioned transformer to predict continuous geometric attributes. This factorization into discrete structure (via VLM) and continuous parameters (via specialized network) enables effective image-to-CAD conversion.
method_summary: Factorizes image-to-CAD into discrete structure prediction (via GPT-4V vision-language model) and continuous attribute prediction (via semantic-conditioned transformer). VLM handles high-level program structure while specialized network handles precise geometry.

title: SolidGen: An Autoregressive Model for Direct B-rep Synthesis
abstract: We present SolidGen, an autoregressive neural network that models the B-rep directly by predicting the vertices, edges, and faces using Transformer-based and pointer neural networks. The approach enables learning and synthesizing boundary representations without requiring supervision through CAD modeling sequences. A key innovation is the Indexed Boundary Representation, which references B-rep vertices, edges and faces in a well-defined hierarchy for machine learning. The model supports conditioning on class labels, images, and voxels.
method_summary: Autoregressive Transformer with pointer networks that directly generates B-rep elements (vertices, edges, faces) in sequence. Introduces Indexed Boundary Representation for ML-friendly B-rep encoding. First direct B-rep synthesis without CAD command sequences.

title: CAD-MLLM: Unifying Multimodality-Conditioned CAD Generation With MLLM
abstract: We present a unified CAD generation system capable of producing parametric CAD models from diverse user inputs including text descriptions, images, point clouds, or combinations thereof. The framework leverages command sequences and large language models to align multimodal data with CAD representations. We introduce Omni-CAD, the first multimodal CAD dataset containing textual description, multi-view images, points, and command sequence for each CAD model with approximately 450K instances.
method_summary: Multimodal LLM-based CAD generation system that aligns text, image, and point cloud inputs with CAD command sequences. Frozen encoders with projection layers map each modality to LLM token space. Introduces Omni-CAD dataset (450K multimodal CAD instances).

title: ExtrudeNet: Unsupervised Inverse Sketch-and-Extrude for Shape Parsing
abstract: Sketch-and-extrude is a common and intuitive modeling process in computer aided design. This paper studies the problem of learning the shape given in the form of point clouds by inverse sketch-and-extrude. We introduce ExtrudeNet, an unsupervised network that discovers sketch and extrude operations from point clouds. Key contributions include a representation supporting freeform sketches and conventional primitives, plus a numerical method for computing signed distance fields.
method_summary: Unsupervised network that decomposes point clouds into sketch-and-extrude operations. Supports freeform sketches and conventional primitives (cylinders, boxes). Produces compact, editable CAD-compatible representations without supervision.

title: AGDC: Autoregressive Generation of Variable-Length Sequences with Joint Discrete and Continuous Spaces
abstract: Transformer-based autoregressive models excel in data generation but are inherently constrained by their reliance on discretized tokens, which limits their ability to represent continuous values with high precision. We propose AGDC, a framework combining categorical prediction for discrete values with diffusion-based modeling for continuous values. Key innovations include end-of-sequence logit adjustment and length regularization. We introduce ContLayNet, a large-scale benchmark with 334K semiconductor layout samples.
method_summary: Unified framework combining categorical prediction for discrete tokens with diffusion-based generation for continuous values in autoregressive sequences. Handles variable-length sequences with mixed discrete-continuous data types. Applicable to CAD-like parametric sequences.

title: Text2CAD: Generating Sequential CAD Models from Beginner-to-Expert Level Text Prompts
abstract: We propose Text2CAD, the first AI framework for generating text-to-parametric CAD models using designer-friendly instructions for all skill levels. We introduce a data annotation pipeline producing approximately 170,000 models with 660,000 text annotations, ranging from abstract CAD descriptions to detailed specifications. The framework employs an end-to-end transformer-based auto-regressive network to convert text inputs into parametric CAD models.
method_summary: End-to-end transformer-based autoregressive network that converts multi-level text prompts into parametric CAD command sequences. Uses a data annotation pipeline with Mistral and LLaVA-NeXT to create 170K models with 660K text annotations at varying detail levels.

title: CAD-Coder: Text-to-CAD Generation with Chain-of-Thought and Geometric Reward
abstract: We introduce CAD-Coder, a novel framework that reformulates text-to-CAD as the generation of CadQuery scripts, a Python-based parametric CAD language. This representation enables direct geometric validation and integrates with existing LLMs. We employ a two-stage pipeline combining supervised fine-tuning on paired text-CadQuery data with reinforcement learning using Group Reward Policy Optimization, guided by rewards measuring both geometric fidelity and format correctness. We introduce a chain-of-thought planning process and construct a dataset comprising 110K text-CadQuery-3D model triplets and 1.5K CoT samples.
method_summary: Two-stage pipeline: SFT on text-CadQuery pairs followed by RL with GRPO using geometric fidelity and format rewards. Introduces chain-of-thought planning for structured CAD reasoning. Generates CadQuery Python scripts directly.

title: Zero-to-CAD: Agentic Synthesis of Interpretable CAD Programs at Million-Scale Without Real Data
abstract: We introduce Zero-to-CAD, a framework that synthesizes executable CAD sequences by embedding a large language model within a feedback-driven CAD environment to iteratively generate and validate code. It produces approximately one million executable CAD sequences and a curated subset of 100K high-quality models. Fine-tuning a vision-language model on synthetic data to reconstruct editable CAD programs from images outperforms strong baselines including GPT-5.2.
method_summary: Agentic search framework embedding LLM in feedback-driven CAD environment for iterative code generation and validation. Produces 1M synthetic CAD sequences without real data. Downstream VLM fine-tuning for image-to-CAD reconstruction.

title: FutureCAD: Towards High-Fidelity CAD Generation via LLM-Driven Program Generation and Text-Based B-Rep Primitive Grounding
abstract: We present FutureCAD, a framework bridging parametric CAD modeling and boundary representation synthesis. It leverages LLMs and a B-Rep grounding transformer (BRepGround) to generate executable CadQuery scripts, enabling natural language specification of geometric selections. We apply supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning on a new real-world CAD dataset.
method_summary: LLM generates CadQuery scripts with text-based geometric selection queries, grounded by BRepGround transformer to actual B-Rep primitives. Combines SFT + RL training on real-world CAD data for high-fidelity generation.

title: Seek-CAD: A Self-refined Generative Modeling for 3D Parametric CAD Using Local Inference via DeepSeek
abstract: Seek-CAD is the pioneer exploration of locally deployed open-source inference LLM DeepSeek-R1 for CAD parametric model generation with a training-free methodology. It introduces a self-refinement mechanism combining visual feedback and Chain-of-Thought reasoning from a Vision Language Model to iteratively improve generated designs. We present a new 3D CAD dataset organized around the SSR paradigm covering diverse CAD commands.
method_summary: Training-free approach using locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 with self-refinement via visual feedback and VLM-based CoT reasoning. Iteratively improves CAD designs without fine-tuning. Introduces SSR-paradigm dataset.

title: ReCAD: Reinforcement Learning Enhanced Parametric CAD Model Generation with Vision-Language Models
abstract: We present ReCAD, an RL framework that bootstraps pretrained large models to generate precise parametric CAD models from multimodal inputs. We fine-tune vision-language models using parameterized code for accurate textual supervision. A novel RL strategy incorporates parameterized code as guidance with hierarchical primitive learning under a unified reward function balancing geometric accuracy and semantic fidelity.
method_summary: RL framework bootstrapping pretrained VLMs for parametric CAD generation. Uses parameterized code as supervision, hierarchical primitive learning, and unified reward balancing geometric accuracy with semantic fidelity. Achieves major CD reduction on image-to-CAD.

title: cadrille: Multi-modal CAD Reconstruction with Online Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We present cadrille, a multimodal CAD reconstruction model that processes point clouds, images, and text simultaneously. Following LLM training paradigms, we employ supervised fine-tuning on procedurally generated data followed by reinforcement learning using online feedback. Online RL algorithms such as GRPO outperform offline alternatives. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results on three datasets including a real-world benchmark.
method_summary: Multi-modal VLM for CAD reconstruction from point clouds, images, and text. SFT on procedural data + online RL (GRPO) fine-tuning. First exploration of RL fine-tuning for CAD tasks. Generates executable CadQuery Python code.

title: CAD-RL: From Intent to Execution: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for Precise CAD Code Generation
abstract: We introduce CAD-RL, which leverages LLMs to automate parametric 3D modeling through multimodal Chain-of-Thought guided reinforcement learning post training. The framework combines reasoning-based initialization with goal-driven learning using three reward signals: executability, geometric accuracy, and external evaluation. We implement Trust Region Stretch, Precision Token Loss, and Overlong Filtering. We release ExeCAD, a dataset of 16,540 real-world CAD examples.
method_summary: Multimodal CoT-guided RL post-training for CAD code generation. Three-reward system (executability, geometric accuracy, external evaluation). Novel techniques: Trust Region Stretch for exploration, Precision Token Loss, Overlong Filtering. Releases ExeCAD dataset (16.5K).

title: RLCAD: Reinforcement Learning Training Gym for Revolution Involved CAD Command Sequence Generation
abstract: We introduce a reinforcement learning environment built on a CAD geometric engine for generating command sequences from boundary representation geometry. Given an input B-Rep geometry, the policy network generates actions processed through the gym to produce CAD geometry with rewards from geometric differences. The approach extends beyond prior work by supporting revolution operations alongside sketching, Boolean, and extrusion operations.
method_summary: RL training gymnasium built on CAD geometric engine. Policy network generates CAD commands from B-Rep input, receives geometric difference rewards. Extends to revolution operations beyond standard sketch-extrude.

title: Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: This survey examines how LLMs have seen rapid advancements and explores their integration with CAD systems. It outlines CAD's industrial importance, reviews LLM foundations including both proprietary and open-source models, and presents a taxonomy of six key application areas where LLMs impact CAD workflows. The authors also propose future research directions.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of LLM+CAD integration covering six application areas. Reviews proprietary and open-source LLM foundations and proposes future research directions for CAD technology through LLM integration.

title: CAD-Llama: Leveraging Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design Parametric 3D Model Generation
abstract: We investigate using LLMs to generate parametric sequences for CAD models. LLMs neither encounter parametric sequences during pretraining nor possess direct awareness of 3D structures. We introduce CAD-Llama with a hierarchical annotation pipeline and Structured Parametric CAD Code (SPCC) format. The framework combines adaptive pretraining on SPCC with instruction tuning aligned to CAD-specific guidelines.
method_summary: Hierarchical annotation pipeline + Structured Parametric CAD Code (SPCC) format to bridge LLM pretraining gap. Adaptive pretraining on SPCC + instruction tuning for CAD-specific spatial awareness.

title: CADCodeVerify: Generating CAD Code with Vision-Language Models for 3D Designs
abstract: We introduce CADCodeVerify, a method for iteratively verifying and improving 3D objects generated from CAD code. The approach uses VLMs to generate and answer validation questions to verify the generated object and prompt the VLM to correct deviations. We present CADPrompt, a benchmark with 200 natural language prompts paired with expert-annotated CAD scripting code.
method_summary: Iterative verification loop using VLMs to generate validation questions, check generated CAD objects, and correct deviations. Introduces CADPrompt benchmark (200 prompts with expert annotations).

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: We present CAD-GPT which introduces a 3D Modeling Spatial Mechanism that maps spatial positions and sketch plane rotations into linguistic feature space using a specialized unfolding mechanism, while discretizing 2D sketch coordinates for precise spatial determination. The method accepts either images or text as input.
method_summary: 3D Modeling Spatial Mechanism mapping spatial positions and rotations into linguistic feature space. Unfolding mechanism + coordinate discretization for precise spatial reasoning in multimodal LLMs.

title: ProCAD: Clarify Before You Draw: Proactive Agents for Robust Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: We introduce ProCAD, a proactive framework addressing specification issues before code synthesis. It combines a clarifying agent that audits prompts and poses targeted questions with a CAD coding agent translating specifications into executable CadQuery programs. ProCAD surpasses frontier models including Claude Sonnet 4.5, achieving 79.9% reduction in mean Chamfer distance.
method_summary: Proactive two-agent framework: clarifying agent audits prompts for ambiguity/conflicts before CAD coding agent generates CadQuery code. Agentic SFT on clarification trajectories. Addresses robustness to ambiguous inputs.

title: ToolCAD: Exploring Tool-Using Large Language Models in Text-to-CAD Generation with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We introduce ToolCAD, which deploys LLMs as tool-using agents for text-to-CAD generation. We develop an interactive CAD modeling environment for generating reasoning trajectories with hybrid feedback and human guidance. An end-to-end post-training strategy enables LLM agents to develop refined CAD Modeling Chain of Thought through online curriculum reinforcement learning.
method_summary: LLM as tool-using agent interacting with CAD engine. Interactive environment generates reasoning trajectories with hybrid feedback. Online curriculum RL for developing CAD Modeling CoT. Bridges open-source LLMs to proprietary model performance.

title: Text-to-CadQuery: A New Paradigm for CAD Generation with Scalable Large Model Capabilities
abstract: We propose direct generation of CadQuery code from natural language, leveraging pretrained LLMs' existing strengths in Python and spatial reasoning. We augmented the Text2CAD dataset with 170,000 CadQuery annotations and fine-tuned six open-source models. The best model achieves a top-1 exact match of 69.3% and reduces Chamfer Distance by 48.6%.
method_summary: Direct CadQuery code generation from text leveraging LLM Python proficiency. Augments Text2CAD with 170K CadQuery annotations. Fine-tunes multiple open-source LLMs showing consistent scaling improvements.

title: Learning From Design Procedure To Generate CAD Programs for Data Augmentation
abstract: We propose a data augmentation paradigm that prompts an LLM to generate CAD programs conditioned on a reference surface program and a modeling procedure, inspired by industrial design practices. By varying reference surfaces using organic shapes, the approach enriches geometric distribution and introduces spline-based curvature features.
method_summary: LLM-based data augmentation for CAD: generates programs conditioned on reference surfaces + modeling procedures. Introduces organic shapes and spline-based features to enrich geometric diversity beyond standard datasets.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: We propose CadVLM, an end-to-end vision language model for CAD generation. Our approach adapts pre-trained foundation models to manipulate engineering sketches effectively, integrating sketch primitives and images. This represents the first instance of a multimodal LLM being successfully applied to parametric CAD generation.
method_summary: First multimodal LLM applied to parametric CAD sketch generation. Adapts pre-trained VLMs to integrate sketch primitives and images for autocompletion, autoconstraint, and image-conditional generation tasks.

title: PLLM: Pseudo-Labeling Large Language Models for CAD Program Synthesis
abstract: We introduce PLLM, a self-training framework for CAD program synthesis from unlabeled 3D shapes. Given a pre-trained CAD-capable LLM and a shape dataset, PLLM iteratively samples candidate programs, selects high-fidelity executions, and augments programs to construct synthetic program-shape pairs for fine-tuning.
method_summary: Self-training framework for CAD program synthesis without paired data. Iterative sampling, selection of high-fidelity executions, and augmentation to create synthetic training pairs from unlabeled 3D shapes.

title: CADEvolve: Creating Realistic CAD via Program Evolution
abstract: We present CADEvolve, an evolution-based pipeline that grows CAD programs from simple primitives using VLM-guided edits and validations. The approach produces 8,000 complex parts as executable CadQuery generators, yielding 1.3 million scripts paired with rendered geometry after post-processing. A fine-tuned VLM achieves state-of-the-art on Image2CAD tasks.
method_summary: Evolution-based pipeline growing CAD programs from primitives via VLM-guided edits and validation. Produces 1.3M CadQuery scripts. Addresses complexity gap in public CAD corpora by generating industrial-grade parts.

title: CAD-Tokenizer: Towards Text-based CAD Prototyping via Modality-Specific Tokenization
abstract: We propose CAD-Tokenizer, which uses a sequence-based VQ-VAE with primitive-level pooling and constrained decoding to produce compact, structure-aware representations. Standard LLM tokenizers fail to capture primitive-level CAD semantics. Applied to unified text-to-CAD generation and editing.
method_summary: Modality-specific tokenization for CAD using VQ-VAE with primitive-level pooling. Captures CAD semantics better than standard LLM tokenizers. Enables unified text-to-CAD generation and editing.

title: OpenECAD: An Efficient Visual Language Model for Editable 3D-CAD Design
abstract: We present OpenECAD, fine-tuned visual language models (0.55B to 3.1B parameters) that transform 3D design images into editable CAD outputs with highly structured 2D sketches and 3D construction commands. Uses dependency-based sketch generation and specialized datasets from public CAD repositories.
method_summary: Efficient VLMs (0.55B-3.1B) fine-tuned for image-to-editable-CAD. Dependency-based sketch generation producing structured 2D sketches and 3D commands compatible with CAD APIs.

title: From Idea to CAD: A Language Model-Driven Multi-Agent System for Collaborative Design
abstract: We present a VLM-based Multi Agent System with access to parametric CAD tooling and tool documentation. Combining agents for requirements engineering, CAD engineering, and vision-based quality assurance, a model is generated automatically from sketches and/or textual descriptions.
method_summary: Multi-agent system mirroring engineering team structure: requirements agent, CAD agent, QA agent. VLM-based with parametric CAD tool access. Iterative refinement with user input.

title: CADDesigner: Conceptual Design of CAD Models Based on General-Purpose Agent
abstract: We present an LLM-powered agent that accepts textual and sketch inputs, refines design requirements through interactive dialogue, and generates CAD code with iterative visual feedback. Built on the Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm with a structured knowledge base for continuous improvement.
method_summary: LLM agent with interactive dialogue for requirement refinement. Context-Independent Imperative Paradigm (CIP) for code generation. Iterative visual feedback loop with structured knowledge base.

title: TCADGen: Automated CAD Modeling Sequence Generation from Text Descriptions via Transformer-Based Large Language Models
abstract: We propose a language-guided framework combining LLMs with computer-automated design. Key contributions include a semi-automated data annotation pipeline, a Transformer-based CAD generator (TCADGen) using dual-channel feature aggregation, and CADLLM that refines generated sequences using confidence scores.
method_summary: Transformer-based CAD generator with dual-channel feature aggregation. Semi-automated annotation pipeline using LLMs+VLMs. CADLLM refinement using confidence scores for industrial workflow automation.

title: Generative AI for CAD Automation: Leveraging Large Language Models for 3D Modelling
abstract: We explore how LLMs can streamline CAD workflows by integrating with FreeCAD. LLMs generate initial CAD scripts from natural language descriptions, which are then executed and refined iteratively based on error feedback. Testing across designs of varying complexity shows the approach works well for simpler models but encounters difficulties with highly constrained designs.
method_summary: LLM integration with FreeCAD for iterative script generation and error-feedback refinement. Empirical study across varying complexity levels identifying limitations with constrained designs.

title: Query2CAD: Generating CAD models using natural language queries
abstract: We introduce Query2CAD, a framework leveraging LLMs to generate CAD designs from natural language without supervised training data. Uses LLM as both generator and refiner with BLIP2 feedback and human-in-the-loop corrections. GPT-4 Turbo achieves 53.6% success rate on first attempt with 23.1% improvement through refinements.
method_summary: Training-free LLM-based CAD generation with self-refinement loop. BLIP2 visual feedback + human-in-the-loop corrections. Demonstrates diminishing returns after first refinement iteration.

title: CAD-Judge: Toward Efficient Morphological Grading and Verification for Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: We propose CAD-Judge, a novel verifiable reward system for efficient CAD preference grading and grammatical validation. Uses Compiler-as-a-Judge Module for fast reward signals and Compiler-as-a-Review Module for verification. Employs prospect theory optimization and agentic generation.
method_summary: Verifiable reward system for CAD generation evaluation. Compiler-as-a-Judge for fast rewards + Compiler-as-a-Review for verification. Prospect theory optimization for preference grading.

title: Human-in-the-Loop: Quantitative Evaluation of 3D Models Generation by Large Language Models
abstract: We introduce a human-in-the-loop framework for quantitative evaluation of LLM-generated 3D models. We propose metrics including volumetric accuracy, surface alignment, dimensional fidelity, and topological intricacy. Using an L-bracket case study, we compare LLM performance across four input modalities.
method_summary: Quantitative evaluation framework for LLM-generated CAD models. Metrics: volumetric accuracy, surface alignment, dimensional fidelity, topological intricacy. Compares four input modalities showing code-level prompts achieve perfect reconstruction.
input_modality: multiple (2D views, isometric, structure tree, code)

title: CAD-Coder (MIT): An Open-Source Vision-Language Model for Computer-Aided Design Code Generation
abstract: We introduce CAD-Coder, a fine-tuned VLM that generates editable CAD code in CadQuery Python from visual input. Built on GenCAD-Code dataset with over 163K image-code pairs. Outperforms GPT-4.5 and Qwen2.5-VL-72B, achieving 100% valid syntax rate. Demonstrates generalizability to real-world images.
method_summary: Fine-tuned VLM for image-to-CadQuery code generation. GenCAD-Code dataset (163K image-code pairs). Achieves 100% valid syntax rate, outperforming GPT-4.5 and Qwen2.5-VL-72B.

title: Text2CAD: Text to 3D CAD Generation via Technical Drawings
abstract: We introduce a framework that employs stable diffusion models to automate CAD generation from text. Converts textual specifications into isometric images, then transforms into orthographic views (top, front, side) to reconstruct 3D CAD models while maintaining physical and dimensional consistency.
method_summary: Text -> isometric image (via diffusion) -> orthographic views -> 3D CAD reconstruction pipeline. Uses stable diffusion for intermediate visual representation maintaining engineering consistency.

title: GenCAD-3D: CAD Program Generation using Multimodal Latent Space Alignment and Synthetic Dataset Balancing
abstract: We introduce GenCAD-3D, a multimodal framework using contrastive learning to align latent embeddings between CAD and geometric encoders, combined with latent diffusion models for sequence generation and retrieval. SynthBal, a synthetic data augmentation strategy, balances and expands datasets enhancing complex geometry representation.
method_summary: Contrastive learning aligns CAD and geometric encoder latent spaces. Latent diffusion for sequence generation. SynthBal augmentation strategy for dataset balancing, improving complex geometry performance.

title: Drawing2CAD: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CAD Generation from Vectorized Drawings
abstract: We reframe CAD generation from 2D engineering drawings as a sequence-to-sequence learning problem where vector drawing primitives directly inform parametric CAD operations. Drawing2CAD comprises vector primitive representation, dual-decoder transformer, and soft target distribution loss. We created CAD-VGDrawing paired dataset.
method_summary: Seq2seq learning from vectorized engineering drawings to parametric CAD. Dual-decoder transformer decoupling command type and parameter generation. Soft target distribution loss for parameter flexibility.

title: CADCrafter: Generating Computer-Aided Design Models from Unconstrained Images
abstract: We present CADCrafter, which trains solely on synthetic textureless CAD data while testing on real-world images. A geometry encoder captures diverse geometric features with texture-invariant properties. Since CAD parameter compilation is non-differentiable, we use direct preference optimization with automatic code checker feedback to enforce geometric validity. We collected a real-world dataset of multi-view images paired with CAD command sequences.
method_summary: Image-to-CAD from unconstrained real images. Trains on synthetic textureless data, generalizes to real images via texture-invariant geometry encoder. DPO with code checker feedback for geometric validity.

title: CAD2Program: From 2D CAD Drawings to 3D Parametric Models: A Vision-Language Approach
abstract: We present CAD2Program, a method for converting 2D CAD drawings into 3D parametric models using vision-language techniques. Treats 2D drawings as raster images encoded with a standard Vision Transformer, generating text-based descriptions of 3D parametric models through autoregressive prediction.
method_summary: VLM-based 2D drawing to 3D parametric model conversion. ViT encodes raster drawings, autoregressive text generation for flexible parametric model description. Extends to diverse geometric entities.

title: Image2CADSeq: Computer-Aided Design Sequence and Knowledge Inference from Product Images
abstract: We present a neural network approach that converts product images into CAD operation sequences rather than traditional boundary representation models. CAD sequences offer enhanced flexibility to modify individual steps of model creation. We developed a multi-level evaluation framework and trained on a synthesized dataset.
method_summary: Neural network converting product images to CAD operation sequences (not B-Rep). Multi-level evaluation framework for assessing sequence quality at different granularities.

title: CADReasoner: Iterative Program Editing for CAD Reverse Engineering
abstract: A vision-language model that iteratively refines CAD designs by comparing input shapes with reconstructions and modifying based on discrepancies. Outputs a runnable CadQuery Python program whose rendered mesh is fed back at the next step. Combines multi-view renders and point clouds, employs a scan-simulation protocol to bridge realism gaps, and achieves SOTA results across DeepCAD, Fusion 360, and MCB benchmarks.
method_summary: VLM-based iterative self-editing loop: generates CadQuery code, renders it, compares with target point cloud/multi-view images, and refines over multiple iterations using geometric discrepancy feedback.

title: Point2CAD: Reverse Engineering CAD Models from 3D Point Clouds
abstract: Addresses CAD model reconstruction from point clouds with a hybrid analytic-neural reconstruction scheme combined with a novel implicit neural representation for freeform surfaces. Achieves SOTA on the ABC benchmark by recovering accurate edges and corners through analytic intersection of adjacent surfaces.
method_summary: Hybrid analytic-neural pipeline: segments point cloud, fits geometric primitives, uses implicit neural representation for freeform surfaces, and recovers B-rep topology through analytic surface intersection.

title: TransCAD: A Hierarchical Transformer for CAD Sequence Inference from Point Clouds
abstract: Introduces TransCAD, a transformer-based system that generates CAD sequences from point clouds using hierarchical learning and a loop refiner for sketch parameters. Testing on DeepCAD and Fusion360 datasets demonstrates SOTA performance, supported by a new evaluation metric.
method_summary: Hierarchical transformer that leverages CAD sequence structure with a loop refiner to regress sketch primitive parameters from point clouds.

title: ComplexGen: CAD Reconstruction by B-Rep Chain Complex Generation
abstract: Models CAD reconstruction as detecting geometric primitives (vertices, edges, surface patches) and their relationships as a chain complex. Uses sparse CNN encoder and tri-path transformer decoder to generate primitives with probabilities, then recovers B-Rep structure through optimization with structural constraints.
method_summary: Sparse CNN + tri-path transformer decoder detects B-rep primitives and their topological relationships as a chain complex, followed by constrained optimization for definite B-rep recovery.

key_results.status: More accurate detection and constrained reconstruction

title: CAD-Recode: Reverse Engineering CAD Code from Point Clouds
abstract: Translates point clouds into executable Python code that reconstructs CAD models. Uses a small LLM decoder with a point cloud projector, trained on one million procedurally generated CAD sequences. Outperforms existing approaches and enables LLM-based CAD editing and Q&A from point clouds.
method_summary: Point cloud projector + small LLM decoder generates executable Python CAD code from point clouds, trained on 1M procedurally generated sequences.

key_results.status: Outperforms existing approaches on multiple datasets

title: Point2Primitive: CAD Reconstruction from Point Cloud by Direct Primitive Prediction
abstract: Directly predicts explicit parametric primitives of CAD models from point clouds rather than using implicit neural representations. Treats sketch reconstruction as a set prediction problem with transformer-based decoder, formulating curve parameters as explicit position queries optimized autoregressively.
method_summary: Transformer-based set prediction that directly detects and predicts sketch curve types and parameters from point clouds using explicit position queries, avoiding implicit representations.

key_results.status: Superior primitive accuracy and geometric fidelity vs implicit approaches

title: PS-CAD: Local Geometry Guidance via Prompting and Selection for CAD Reconstruction
abstract: Reconstructs CAD modeling sequences step by step with local geometry guidance. At each step provides geometry of surfaces where the current sketch lies and local point cloud geometry as prompts, plus a selection mechanism to choose the best candidate.
method_summary: Step-by-step CAD sequence reconstruction with local geometry prompting (surface geometry + local point cloud) and selection mechanism for candidate evaluation.

title: GACO-CAD: Geometry-Augmented and Conciseness-Optimized CAD Model Generation from Single Image
abstract: Two-stage post-training framework for image-to-CAD: SFT with depth and surface normal maps as geometric priors, then RL with group length reward for compact modeling sequences while maintaining geometric fidelity.
method_summary: SFT with geometric priors (depth + normal maps) followed by RL with group length reward for concise yet accurate CAD generation from single images.

title: View2CAD: Reconstructing View-Centric CAD Models from Single RGB-D Scans
abstract: Reconstructs parametric CAD models from single RGB-D images using a view-centric B-rep representation that handles visibility limits and encodes geometric uncertainty. Combines image segmentation with geometric optimization.
method_summary: View-centric B-rep representation with visibility handling and uncertainty encoding; image segmentation + geometric optimization for partial observation recovery.

key_results.status: Handles partial observations from single views

title: PICASSO: A Feed-Forward Framework for Parametric Inference of CAD Sketches via Rendering Self-Supervision
abstract: Converts CAD sketch images into parametric primitives using rendering self-supervision to pre-train without parameter-level annotations. Comprises Sketch Parameterization Network and differentiable Sketch Rendering Network. Effective in zero- and few-shot scenarios.
method_summary: Feed-forward sketch parameterization with rendering self-supervision: differentiable rendering enables training without parameter annotations for sketch-to-parametric conversion.

title: 3D CAD Model Reconstruction from 2D Sketch using Visual Transformer and Rhino Grasshopper
abstract: Introduces a 3D reconstruction method using visual transformer to predict a scene descriptor from a single wireframe image. The descriptor is then used to generate 3D CAD models through Rhino Grasshopper, producing parametric outputs compatible with CAD software.
method_summary: Visual transformer predicts scene descriptors from wireframe sketches; Rhino Grasshopper generates parametric 3D CAD models from descriptors.

title: CAD2Program: From 2D CAD Drawings to 3D Parametric Models
abstract: Reconstructs 3D parametric models from 2D CAD drawings using VLMs. Encodes 2D drawings as raster images with ViT and auto-regressively generates text describing 3D parametric models. Text-based approach offers greater flexibility than fixed-slot domain-specific sequences.
method_summary: ViT encodes 2D CAD drawings as raster images; auto-regressive text generation describes 3D parametric models, extending to arbitrary geometric entities and semantic properties.

title: Drawing2CAD: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CAD Generation from Vector Drawings
abstract: Frames CAD generation from 2D engineering drawings as sequence-to-sequence learning. Features network-friendly vector primitive representation, dual-decoder transformer for decoupling command and parameter generation, and soft target distribution loss. Introduces CAD-VGDrawing dataset.
method_summary: Dual-decoder transformer: one decoder for CAD commands, another for parameters, with soft target distribution loss. Converts vectorized drawing primitives to parametric CAD operations.

title: CAD Sequence and Knowledge Inference from Product Images
abstract: Infers CAD construction sequences and design knowledge from product images. Addresses scenarios where digital CAD files are not accessible, using data-driven reverse engineering approaches to reconstruct both the modeling steps and underlying design intent.
method_summary: Infers CAD construction sequences and design knowledge directly from product photographs, bridging the gap between visual product data and parametric CAD representations.

title: CADMorph: Geometry-Driven Parametric CAD Editing via a Plan-Generate-Verify Loop
abstract: Iterative plan-generate-verify framework for geometry-driven parametric CAD editing. Orchestrates pretrained domain-specific foundation models (P2S latent diffusion + masked-parameter-prediction) to modify CAD sequences while preserving structure and semantic validity. Outperforms GPT-4o and specialized baselines.
method_summary: Plan-Generate-Verify loop: plans edits based on geometry changes, generates modified CAD sequences using pretrained P2S diffusion and masked-parameter models, verifies shape fidelity.

title: CADKnitter: Compositional CAD Generation from Text and Geometry Guidance
abstract: Compositional CAD generation framework producing complementary parts that assemble according to text descriptions and geometric constraints. Releases KnitCAD dataset (310K+ CAD samples with text descriptions and assembly metadata).
method_summary: Geometry-guided diffusion sampling generates complementary CAD parts that fit geometrically with existing parts and align semantically with text prompts.

title: ArtiCAD: Articulated CAD Assembly Design via Multi-Agent Code Generation
abstract: First training-free multi-agent system for generating editable, articulated CAD assemblies from text or images. Four specialized agents with Connector for attachment points and joint parameters. Cross-stage rollback mechanism and self-evolving experience store. Supports URDF export.
method_summary: Multi-agent system: four specialized agents divide assembly design task, Connector predicts attachment points and joint parameters, cross-stage rollback corrects errors, experience store enables self-improvement.

key_results.status: First articulated CAD assembly generation system

title: AADvark: Agent-Aided Design for Dynamic CAD Models
abstract: Agentic system for designing 3D assemblies with moving parts (pistons, scissors, etc.). Captures dynamic part interactions with degrees-of-freedom and integrates constraint solvers with specialized visual feedback for spatial reasoning.
method_summary: Agent-in-the-loop system with code generation, CAD compilation, visualization, and iterative refinement. Integrates constraint solvers for dynamic part interactions with DOF.

key_results.status: First system for dynamic CAD assemblies with moving parts

title: JoinABLe: Learning Bottom-up Assembly of Parametric CAD Joints
abstract: Learning-based method for assembling CAD parts into joints using weak supervision from parametric CAD files. Makes predictions over graph representation of solid models with 79.53% accuracy approaching human performance (80%). Releases Fusion 360 Gallery assembly dataset.
method_summary: Graph neural network over solid model B-rep faces/edges predicts joint types and parameters for bottom-up assembly, using weak supervision from CAD file metadata.
